key_themes: Strong Q1 2026 earnings expectations with potential EPS upside surprise
Accelerating shift to smoke-free products (IQOS heated tobacco and ZYN nicotine pouches)
Analyst consensus of Moderate Buy with average price targets ~$195 (≈24% upside from ~$158)
Attractive dividend yield, cash flow growth, and defensive valuation
Recent modest PT cuts and YTD stock pullback amid FX/regulatory notes, but sentiment stabilizing ahead of earnings
summary: Philip Morris International (PM) enters its April 22, 2026 Q1 earnings with positive momentum. Wall Street expects ~8% EPS growth, and several analysts forecast an upside surprise that could catalyze the stock, which has been somewhat lackluster YTD despite strong long-term fundamentals.[[1]](https://seekingalpha.com/article/4891483-philip-morris-expect-upside-surprise-from-q1-2026-results)[[2]](https://www.zacks.com/stock/news/2900676/philip-morris-pm-reports-next-week-wall-street-expects-earnings-growth) The ongoing transformation toward smoke-free offerings (IQOS relaunch in the US, ZYN growth) remains the dominant driver, bolstered by robust pricing power, double-digit cash-flow growth, and a ~4.8–5% dividend yield. While a handful of recent price-target reductions (e.g., MS to $190, UBS to $168) reflect FX and regulatory caution around nicotine pouches, the overall analyst consensus stays solidly bullish with 20–25% average upside from current levels near $158. Investor chatter on X highlights stabilized sentiment post-pullback, historical outperformance, and keen focus on the upcoming ZYN/IQOS metrics.[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/PM/forecast/)[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PM/history/)
sources: 15+ news/analyst articles (Seeking Alpha, Zacks, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat), 20+ relevant X posts (semantic + keyword searches since Jan 2026), Multiple analyst ratings (9–12 firms showing predominantly Buy/Overweight)
